name.$comment: About | Name | Product or coding-agent surface.

links.$comment: About | Links | Product links and stable slug.

form_factor.$comment: About | Form Factor | CLI, IDE, extension, terminal UI, hosted agent, or hybrid.

released_in.$comment: About | Released | First public release month and year for this surface.

latest_major_update.$comment: About | Latest Major Update | Most recent major public update captured in this dataset.

first_party_coding_model_plans.$comment: About | Model Plan Pricing | Pricing for native first-party coding/model access plans, or BYOK/local-only status when no first-party plan is sold.

some_free_usage.$comment: Access | Some Free Usage | Has a documented free tier, free mode, free included usage, or local/free-model path for agent work.

no_account_required.$comment: Access | No Account Required | Can be used for agent work without signing into the product/vendor account, typically via local models.

can_use_other_subscriptions.$comment: Access | Can Use Other Subscriptions | Can route agent usage through another subscription, provider account, API key, or local inference backend.

rules.$comment: Context | Rules | Persistent project or global instruction files.

skills.$comment: Context | Skills | Reusable skill folders, commands, playbooks, or agent capabilities.

hooks.$comment: Automation | Hooks | User-configurable lifecycle hooks or event hooks.

mcp_servers.$comment: Integration | MCP | Model Context Protocol server support.

goal_command.$comment: Automation | /goal | Built-in `/goal` slash command for a persistent session objective that auto-continues until a completion condition is met (or pause/clear).

subagents.$comment: Automation | Subagents | First-class custom or delegated subagent support.

monitor.$comment: Automation | Monitor | Background monitor/watch capability that lets the agent wait on, observe, or react to long-running commands, subagents, jobs, logs, WebSockets, or task status changes without blocking normal interaction.

transcripts.$comment: Context | Transcripts | Local or exported conversation/session transcript storage.

model_selection.$comment: Settings | Model | Explicit model selection in CLI/settings.

approval_mode.$comment: Settings | Approvals | Approval or permission-mode controls.

resume.$comment: Settings | Resume | Resume or continue previous sessions.

continue.$comment: Settings | Continue | Continue the latest/current session without specifying an ID.

output_format.$comment: Settings | Output Format | Machine-readable or alternate output format controls.

statusline.$comment: Settings | Status Line | User-configurable CLI status line or equivalent.

keyboard_shortcuts.$comment: Settings | Keybindings | User-customizable keyboard shortcuts or keybindings.

theme_customization.$comment: Settings | Theme | Dark/light mode, custom themes, or UI color scheme customization.

auto_save.$comment: Settings | Auto-Save | Automatic file or session save behavior controls.

debugging_output.$comment: Settings | Debug Output | Verbose logging, debug mode, or diagnostic output controls.

context_window.$comment: Settings | Context Window | Configurable context length or token limits.

code_formatting.$comment: Settings | Code Formatting | Built-in formatter/linter integration or customization.

git_integration.$comment: Settings | Git Integration | Git command support, diff viewing, branch management.

performance_mode.$comment: Settings | Performance | Caching, optimization, or performance tuning controls.

ui_customization.$comment: Settings | UI Customization | Sidebar, layout, panel arrangement customization.

hosted_agent.$comment: Platform | Hosted Agent | Managed cloud/hosted agent platform.

custom_model_provider.$comment: Models | Arbitrary Models | Ability to plug in arbitrary model providers or OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

notes.$comment: About | Notes | Short caveats for interpretation.

sort_date.$comment: ISO date for column sorting (often set at bundle time from GitHub).

links.$comment: Per-form-factor install/download links keyed by display value, e.g. CLI, IDE, Extension, SDK, Web, Mac App.
